Thought 4: The narrative also mentions that Floyd was preparing to alter his will, and Camille had the updated will paperwork in her possession. This suggests she might have known about the changes and had a motive to prevent them or benefit from the previous will. Additionally, her financial troubles (loan, spending habits) provide a strong motive. Combined with her presence at the crime scene, her ownership and proficiency with a shotgun, and the fact that she was the only one on the mountain with Floyd, the evidence strongly points to Camille as the murderer. Francis has a motive but no clear means (shotgun access) or opportunity (no confirmation he was on the mountain). Therefore, Camille is the most likely suspect.
